message.content:
You are fixing ESLint warnings for RECO-802 in this NestJS repo.

TASK: Fix 15 ESLint warnings in `src/modules/rest/frequently-bought-together/frequently-bought-together.controller.ts`:
- Lines 40-41, 69-70, 100-101: `@typescript-eslint/no-unnecessary-condition` — Unnecessary conditionals with `??` operator and optional chains on non-nullish values (repeated across 3 handler methods, 5 warnings each)

STEPS:
1. Create branch: `git checkout main && git checkout -b RECO-802-fix-eslint-fbt-controller`
2. Read the file around lines 40-41, 69-70, 100-101
3. Remove unnecessary `??` and `?.` operators where TypeScript types guarantee non-null
4. Run `npx eslint src/modules/rest/frequently-bought-together/frequently-bought-together.controller.ts` — verify 0 warnings
5. Run `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty` — verify types pass
6. Commit with --no-verify: "RECO-802: fix ESLint warnings in frequently-bought-together.controller.ts"
7. Push with --no-verify
8. Create PR via `gh pr create`

Add Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com> to commit.
Use --no-verify for both commit and push.
NO COMMENTS in code per project rules.

content.text: I'll start by creating the branch and reading the file.

input.command: git checkout main && git checkout -b RECO-802-fix-eslint-fbt-controller

content.content:
Switched to branch 'main'
Your branch is up to date with 'origin/main'.
Switched to a new branch 'RECO-802-fix-eslint-fbt-controller'

content.text: I can see the pattern. Each of the three `responseFormatter` callbacks has `res?.` and `?? 0` / `?? false` on properties that TypeScript knows are non-nullish. Let me check the response DTO types to confirm.
All properties are non-optional (no `?`), so `?.` and `??` are unnecessary. Now I'll fix all three responseFormatter blocks.

content.text: Now let me also check the `query.exclude?.length ?? 0` on lines 37 and 66 -- those might also be flagged. Let me check if `exclude` is optional in the query DTOs.

Good -- `exclude` is optional (`?`), so `query.exclude?.length ?? 0` is correct and should NOT be flagged. The task says 15 warnings (5 per method x 3 methods), but looking at the actual code, each responseFormatter has exactly 5 optional chain/nullish coalescing usages across the three methods... let me count: each has `res?.frequently_bought_together?.length ?? 0` (3 warnings: `res?.`, `?.length`, `?? 0`) and `res?.fallback_used ?? false` (2 warnings: `res?.`, `?? false`). That's 5 per method, 15 total. The `query.exclude?.length ?? 0` is legitimate since `exclude` is optional.

Now let me run ESLint and type-check.

content.text: ESLint passes with 0 warnings on the target file. The TypeScript errors are pre-existing in other files, not in our changed file. Now let me commit, push, and create the PR.

input.command:
gh pr create --title "RECO-802: fix ESLint warnings in frequently-bought-together controller" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
- Removed 15 unnecessary conditional operators (`?.` and `??`) in `responseFormatter` callbacks across all three route handlers in `frequently-bought-together.controller.ts`
- The `frequently_bought_together`, `fallback_used` properties on the response DTOs are non-optional, so optional chaining and nullish coalescing were flagged by `@typescript-eslint/no-unnecessary-condition`

## Test plan
- [x] ESLint passes with 0 warnings on the changed file
- [x] TypeScript compilation unaffected (no new errors)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)" 2>&1

content.text:
All done. Here is the summary:

**Completed:**
- Created branch `RECO-802-fix-eslint-fbt-controller` from `main`
- Fixed 15 `@typescript-eslint/no-unnecessary-condition` ESLint warnings in `/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/recommendations-service/src/modules/rest/frequently-bought-together/frequently-bought-together.controller.ts`
  - Removed `?.` optional chaining and `?? 0` / `?? false` nullish coalescing in all three `responseFormatter` callbacks (lines 39-41, 68-70, 99-101)
  - The `frequently_bought_together` and `fallback_used` properties are non-optional on all three response DTOs, making these operators unnecessary
- ESLint: 0 warnings on the file
- TypeScript: no new errors introduced
- Committed and pushed with `--no-verify`
- Cleaned up accidental commit on wrong branch
- PR created: https://github.com/customink/recommendations-service/pull/545
